Semiconductor fabrication process node (technology node) of the die being ordered (e.g., 5nm, 7nm, 16nm FinFET, 28nm). Critical for die bank segregation, quality traceability, and export control classification.
Customer-requested date by which the packaged or bare die shipment must be delivered. Used for OSAT scheduling, just-in-time packaging workflow planning, and on-time delivery KPI measurement.
Number of individual dies (Known Good Die units) requested by the customer or internal order for drawdown from the die bank. Drives OSAT packaging run sizing and inventory reservation.

business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the die and its packaging materials comply with the EU RoHS Directive restricting hazardous substances. Required for shipments to EU customers and for customer compliance declarations.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country for the shipment. Critical for export control screening (ITAR/EAR), RoHS/REACH compliance declarations, and customs documentation.
Carrier-assigned tracking number for the outbound shipment of packaged dies. Enables end-to-end shipment visibility and proof-of-delivery confirmation for order fulfillment closure.
Actual number of dies (post-packaging or in bare die form) confirmed as shipped to the customer. Used for fulfillment confirmation, revenue recognition, and DPPM tracking.
Agreed commercial price per die unit for this order, as captured in the SAP SD pricing condition. Used for revenue recognition, margin analysis, and customer invoice generation.
System timestamp of the most recent modification to the die bank order record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.

Identifier of the wafer fabrication facility (FAB) where the source lot was manufactured. Relevant for quality traceability, site-specific yield analysis, and regulatory compliance (ITAR/EAR site-of-manufacture declarations).
Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a specific lot-to-order-line assignment record within the order-to-cash fulfillment process.
Reference to the customer for whom this lot assignment is made. Supports customer-level lot traceability reporting required by automotive and aerospace customers.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Lot assignment ownership links a responsible employee to each wafer lot for traceability and quality audits.

Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: REQUIRED: Lot assignment process records which wafer lot is assigned to a specific order/line; FK provides direct lookup for assignment audits.

Reference to the MPW (Multi-Project Wafer) shuttle run from which this lot originates, applicable for MPW lot types. Links the assignment back to the specific MPW shuttle for design-to-delivery traceability.
Reference to the specific order line item within the sales order for which this lot is being assigned. Enables line-level lot traceability for multi-line orders.

business_glossary_term: Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test (OSAT) Vendor ID
description: Reference to the OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) vendor responsible for packaging and assembly of the lot, if applicable. Relevant for packaged goods lots and KGD lots sourced from external assembly houses.
Reference to the parent sales order header to which this lot assignment belongs. Links the lot assignment back to the customer order for order-to-cash traceability.
Reference to the outbound shipment record under which this lot assignment was physically dispatched. Links the lot assignment forward to the shipment for end-to-end order-to-cash traceability.

Reference to the ATE (Automatic Test Equipment) test program used to qualify the lot prior to assignment. Enables traceability from the customer order back to the specific test program version used for lot qualification.

The actual calendar date on which the assigned lot was physically shipped to the customer. Populated upon shipment confirmation from the MES or SAP SD goods issue posting. Used for on-time delivery (OTD) performance measurement.

Numeric priority rank governing the order in which this lot assignment is fulfilled when supply is constrained. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used by supply chain planners for backlog management and allocation decisions.

The quantity of units from this lot assigned to the order line. For wafer lots this represents wafer count; for die lots this represents die count; for packaged goods lots this represents unit count. Must not exceed the available lot quantity.
The calendar date on which the lot was formally assigned to the customer order line. Used for backlog management, delivery schedule tracking, and IATF 16949 lot traceability audit trails.

Indicates how the lot assignment was created. Manual indicates a planner manually assigned the lot; atp_auto indicates the SAP ATP (Available-to-Promise) engine automatically assigned it; allocation_run indicates it was created by a batch allocation run; planner_override indicates a planner overrode an automatic assignment; system_transfer indicates the assignment was transferred from another order.

Current lifecycle state of the lot-to-order assignment. Tentative indicates a preliminary reservation pending confirmation; confirmed indicates a firm allocation; allocated indicates inventory has been committed; shipped indicates the lot has been dispatched; cancelled indicates the assignment was voided; on_hold indicates the assignment is suspended pending quality disposition or export review.

Indicates whether this lot assignment is part of the open order backlog, meaning the order has not yet been fully shipped and revenue has not been recognized. Used for backlog reporting and revenue forecasting.
The reason code or description explaining why a lot assignment was cancelled. Populated only when assignment_status is cancelled. Used for root cause analysis and supply chain performance reporting.

The certificate of conformance document number issued for this lot assignment, confirming the lot meets all specified quality and regulatory requirements. Required by automotive (IATF 16949) and aerospace customers.

Indicates whether the lot assignment is associated with production eligible for US CHIPS and Science Act incentives or reporting requirements. Relevant for domestic semiconductor manufacturing compliance and subsidy tracking.
The confirmed date on which the assigned lot is scheduled to ship to the customer. Drives delivery commitment and ATP (Available-to-Promise) calculations in SAP SD.

The three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the country where the lot was manufactured. Required for customs declarations, tariff classification, and CHIPS Act domestic content reporting.
The date and time when this lot assignment record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation timestamp required for SOX compliance and IATF 16949 traceability records.
The customer-provided purchase order number associated with this lot assignment. Required for invoice matching, customer receiving, and lot traceability documentation provided to the customer.

The manufacturing date code of the lot in YYYYWW format (year and work week), indicating when the lot was fabricated. Required for component traceability, shelf-life management, and customer receiving inspection.
The defective parts per million rate measured for the assigned lot. Key quality metric reported to automotive customers under IATF 16949 and AIAG quality reporting requirements.

The Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) applicable to the lot being assigned, as required under EAR (Export Administration Regulations). Determines whether an export license is required for shipment to the customer destination country.
The export license number issued by BIS or DDTC authorizing shipment of this lot to the customer, where required. Must be documented on shipping documentation for EAR/ITAR compliance.
Indicates whether an export license is required for shipment of this lot to the customer destination country based on the ECCN and destination. Drives export compliance workflow in SAP GTS.
The SAP QM inspection lot number associated with the quality inspection results for this manufacturing lot. Links the lot assignment to the formal quality inspection record for audit traceability.

business_glossary_term: International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) Controlled Flag
description: Indicates whether the lot being assigned is subject to ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) controls. ITAR-controlled lots require additional compliance review and documentation before shipment.

The KGD (Known Good Die) quality grade assigned to the lot, applicable for die lots and advanced packaging orders. Grade A represents highest quality fully tested die; Grade B represents die meeting relaxed specifications; engineering sample and qualification grades apply to pre-production lots.

The manufacturing lot identifier assigned by the Camstar MES or SAP PP module. Uniquely identifies the wafer lot, die lot, or packaged goods lot being assigned to the order line. This is the primary traceability key linking customer orders back to wafer fabrication.

Classification of the lot being assigned to the order line. Distinguishes between wafer lots (silicon substrates in fabrication), die lots (singulated Known Good Die), packaged goods lots (finished assembled units), KGD (Known Good Die) lots for advanced packaging, and MPW (Multi-Project Wafer) shuttle lots.
The finished goods or intermediate part number (SAP material number) associated with the lot being assigned. Identifies the specific IC, die, or wafer product being fulfilled against the order line.
The revision level of the part number associated with the assigned lot. Critical for automotive and aerospace customers who require revision-level traceability per IATF 16949 and AS9100 requirements.

The semiconductor fabrication process node (technology node) used to manufacture the lot, expressed in nanometers (e.g., 7nm, 5nm, 28nm, 65nm). Identifies the manufacturing technology generation for the assigned lot.

The quality disposition status of the lot at the time of assignment. Accepted indicates the lot passed all quality gates; conditionally accepted indicates the lot is approved with documented deviations; rejected indicates the lot failed quality inspection; under review indicates quality evaluation is in progress; quarantined indicates the lot is isolated pending investigation.

Free-text notes documenting any lot-specific quality disposition details, deviations, waivers, or special handling instructions associated with this assignment. Required for automotive customers under IATF 16949 non-conforming product management.

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the assigned lot is compliant with EU REACH Regulation (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals). Required for EU market shipments and chemical substance reporting.

business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the assigned lot is compliant with the EU RoHS Directive (Restriction of Hazardous Substances), restricting the use of specific hazardous materials in electronic equipment. Required for EU market shipments.
The unit of measure applicable to the assigned quantity. Wafers for wafer lots, dies for die lots, units for packaged goods, reels or trays for tape-and-reel or tray-packaged finished goods.
The date and time when this lot assignment record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to the assignment for audit trail and data lineage purposes in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

The fabrication facility site code where the wafer lot was manufactured. Identifies the specific FAB (Fabrication Facility) for supply chain traceability, export control compliance, and CHIPS Act reporting.

The die yield percentage of the assigned lot, representing the proportion of good dies per wafer that passed all test and inspection criteria. Critical quality metric for automotive and aerospace customers requiring lot-level quality data.
Unique system-generated identifier for the MPW order record. Primary key for the mpw_order data product within the order domain.
Reference to the fabless customer or design house placing this MPW order. Links to the customer master record for billing, contract terms, and design-win tracking in Salesforce CRM.
Reference to the billing invoice generated for this MPW order in SAP S/4HANA FI. Links the order to the accounts receivable and revenue recognition records for financial close and SOX compliance.
Reference to the master agreement or NRE contract governing this MPW order, including pricing, IP ownership, and delivery terms. Links to the contract master in SAP S/4HANA or Oracle Agile PLM.

Reference to the internal sales representative or account manager responsible for this MPW order. Used for commission tracking, design-win attribution, and customer relationship management in Salesforce CRM.

Externally-known identifier for the shared wafer shuttle run in which this MPW order participates. Used to group all customer designs co-fabricated on the same wafer lot. Sourced from the fab or MPW program operator (e.g., TSMC shuttle run code, GlobalFoundries MPW run number).
FK to order.order.order_id — MPW orders are a specialized order type linked to the master order record for customer and commercial terms.
Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: MPW order belongs to a specific sales order; adding child-to-parent FK enables order‑level reporting and removes duplicate order_number.
Reference to the tapeout record (final GDSII design submission) associated with this MPW order. Links the order to the design domain tapeout product for design-to-manufacturing traceability.
Actual date on which the ordered dies were delivered to the customer. Used to measure on-time delivery performance, close the order-to-cash cycle, and trigger final revenue recognition.
System timestamp when the MPW order record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA measurement from order entry.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the order value and NRE charges (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Supports multi-currency billing for international fabless customers.
Name or code of the customer's IC design (chip project) being fabricated in this MPW slot. Used for design-to-order traceability, customer communication, and design-win reporting in Salesforce CRM.

Version or revision identifier of the customer's IC design submitted for this MPW run (e.g., v1.0, Rev-B, tapeout_2). Ensures the correct GDSII version is linked to the order for traceability and re-spin management.

Actual number of known-good dies (KGD) delivered to the customer from this MPW order. May differ from die_quantity_ordered due to yield loss. Used for fulfillment reconciliation and yield-adjusted billing.
Number of known-good dies (KGD) ordered by the customer from this MPW shuttle run. Used for delivery planning, yield-adjusted fulfillment, and revenue recognition.

Export Administration Regulations (EAR) Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) applicable to the IC design in this MPW order. Required for export license determination and BIS/EAR compliance reporting. Example values: EAR99, 3E001, 3A001.

Identifier for the wafer fabrication facility (FAB) where this MPW shuttle run is manufactured (e.g., internal fab or OSAT partner site code). Used for capacity planning, yield tracking, and export compliance (ITAR/EAR).

business_glossary_term: International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) Flag
description: Indicates whether this MPW order involves ITAR-controlled technology requiring State Department licensing and restricted handling. True = ITAR-controlled; False = not ITAR-controlled. Drives access controls and shipment documentation requirements.
Fab-assigned wafer lot number for the MPW shuttle run associated with this order. Used for WIP tracking in Camstar MES, yield analysis, and defect traceability through the fabrication process.

Classification of the MPW shuttle program under which this order is placed. Determines pricing tiers, slot priority, NRE subsidies, and export control applicability (e.g., government programs may require ITAR review).
FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — MPW orders may be linked to a parent sales order for billing and customer relationship tracking.
FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — MPW orders are a specialized order type that should link back to the parent sales order for billing and customer relationship tracking.

One-time Non-Recurring Engineering charge billed to the customer for mask set preparation, reticle slot allocation, and design-in support for this MPW order. Expressed in US dollars. Shared proportionally among shuttle participants based on reticle slot area.
The calendar date on which the customer formally placed the MPW order. Represents the principal business event date for order-to-cash lifecycle tracking and backlog reporting.
Free-text field for capturing special instructions, customer-specific requirements, design constraints, or internal processing notes associated with this MPW order. Not used for structured data.

Current lifecycle state of the MPW order from initial entry through fabrication completion. Drives backlog management, revenue recognition, and customer communication workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|confirmed|tapeout_received|wafer_started|in_fab|completed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
Total monetary value of this MPW order in US dollars, comprising NRE charges plus die unit price multiplied by ordered quantity. Used for backlog reporting, revenue forecasting, and SAP FI billing.

Semiconductor package type requested for the delivered dies (e.g., bare die/KGD, WLCSP, BGA, QFN, flip-chip). Determines OSAT engagement, packaging cost, and delivery lead time. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bare_die|WLCSP|BGA|QFN|flip_chip|CSP — promote to reference product]

Committed date by which the ordered dies are planned to be delivered to the customer after wafer fabrication, dicing, and optional packaging. Used for customer delivery scheduling and order-to-cash SLA tracking.
Customer-issued purchase order number referencing this MPW order. Required for invoice matching, accounts receivable, and customer procurement compliance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD order entry.
Business priority assigned to this MPW order for slot allocation, fab scheduling, and customer support escalation. Critical orders receive expedited processing and dedicated account management.

Semiconductor fabrication process technology node used for this MPW shuttle run (e.g., 5nm, 7nm, 28nm, 65nm). Determines the PDK, design rules, and fab site eligibility. Critical for yield modeling and NRE pricing.

Physical area in square millimeters allocated to this customer's design within the shared MPW reticle. Determines die size, die-per-wafer count, and proportional NRE cost allocation among shuttle participants.

The specific slot number assigned to this customer's design on the shared MPW reticle. Each slot represents a distinct die area on the shared mask set. Slot allocation drives NRE cost sharing and die quantity calculations.

Indicates whether the materials and processes used in this MPW order comply with the EU RoHS Directive restricting hazardous substances in electronic equipment. Required for EU market access and customer compliance declarations.
Geographic sales region code associated with this MPW order (e.g., AMER, EMEA, APAC, JAPAN). Used for regional revenue reporting, quota management, and export control jurisdiction determination.

Contractual deadline by which the customer must submit the final GDSII design data (tapeout) to secure their reticle slot in the MPW shuttle run. Missing this date results in slot forfeiture and potential NRE penalty.
Actual date on which the customer's final GDSII tapeout data was received and accepted by the fab or design services team. Used to confirm slot lock and trigger wafer start authorization.
Per-die price charged to the customer for the ordered die quantity from this MPW run. Expressed in US dollars. Used for revenue recognition, invoice generation, and margin analysis.
System timestamp of the most recent modification to the MPW order record. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.
Number of wafers allocated to this customer's design within the MPW shuttle run. Determines the maximum achievable die yield and is used for fab capacity reservation and WIP tracking.
Actual date on which wafer fabrication was initiated for this MPW shuttle run. Compared against planned wafer start date to measure schedule adherence and TTM performance.
Planned date for initiating wafer fabrication (wafer start) for this MPW shuttle run at the fab site. Used for fab capacity scheduling, WIP planning, and customer delivery commitment.

Percentage of good dies produced relative to the total possible dies from the allocated wafer area in this MPW run. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Used for quality reporting, customer communication, and yield improvement programs.
Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a wafer start authorization record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

Reference to the customer account on whose behalf the wafer start is authorized. Supports order-to-cash traceability and customer-level fab demand reporting. Fulfills the PARTY_REFERENCE minimum category for TRANSACTION_HEADER.
Reference to the fabrication facility (FAB) where the authorized wafer starts are to be executed. Determines routing of the demand signal to the correct MES instance.

Reference to the MPW run record that groups multiple customer designs sharing this wafer start. Populated only when is_mpw is True. Enables allocation of wafer cost and die yield across participating customers.
FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Wafer start authorizations are triggered by customer orders. The WSA must reference the originating sales order for demand traceability.

Identifier of the photolithography reticle (mask) set to be used for this wafer start. Critical for EUV/DUV lithography scheduling and mask inventory management. Ensures the correct GDSII-derived mask set is reserved for the lot.
Reference to the semiconductor product (IC, SoC, ASIC, FPGA, etc.) for which wafers are being authorized to start fabrication. Links to the product master in PLM.
Actual calendar date on which wafer fabrication commenced at the fab site, as recorded by the MES. Compared against planned_start_date to measure schedule adherence and on-time start performance.

Organizational approval tier required and applied for this wafer start authorization. Higher-value or hot-lot authorizations may require director or VP approval. Supports SOX internal controls and delegation-of-authority compliance.

Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the wafer start authorization, used across order management, MES, and fab planning systems. Typically formatted as WSA-{SITE}-{SEQUENCE}. This is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category field for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity.

Current lifecycle state of the wafer start authorization record, tracking progression from initial creation through fab release and completion. Fulfills the LIFECYCLE_STATUS minimum category for TRANSACTION_HEADER. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|released_to_fab|in_progress|completed|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]

Date and time when the wafer start authorization was formally approved and issued. This is the principal BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this TRANSACTION_HEADER — the moment the authorization became binding and the demand signal was created.

Name or employee identifier of the person who formally approved and issued the wafer start authorization. Required for SOX compliance and audit trail. Typically a fab planning manager or order management lead.

Number of wafers authorized to start fabrication under this authorization record. This is the primary quantitative demand signal passed from order management to the MES. Fulfills the QUANTITATIVE_RESULT minimum category for this non-monetary TRANSACTION_HEADER.

Reason code explaining why the wafer start authorization was cancelled, when authorization_status is 'cancelled'. Used for backlog management analysis, demand forecasting accuracy improvement, and fab capacity recovery planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|forecast_revision|capacity_constraint|design_change|yield_risk|export_hold|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Indicates whether this wafer start authorization qualifies for US CHIPS and Science Act incentives or domestic production reporting requirements. Relevant for fab sites receiving CHIPS Act funding and for regulatory compliance reporting to the US Department of Commerce.
SAP cost center to which the wafer fabrication costs for this authorization are allocated. Used for internal cost accounting, NRE cost recovery, and fab P&L reporting.

System timestamp when the wafer start authorization record was first created in the source system. Fulfills the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED minimum category for TRANSACTION_HEADER. Used for data lineage and audit trail.

Target number of calendar days from wafer start to wafer out (completion of all fab processing steps). Defined by process node and priority class. Hot lots have compressed cycle time targets. Used for delivery commit date calculation and fab performance measurement.

Version or revision identifier of the IC design (GDS/GDSII tapeout revision) being fabricated in this wafer start. Ensures the correct design revision is linked to the physical lot for traceability from design to silicon.

Reference to the die bank replenishment plan that triggered this authorization when the wafer start is driven by inventory replenishment rather than a direct customer sales order. Nullable when authorization originates from a sales order.

Number of individual die sites on each wafer for the specified product and process node. Determined by die size and wafer diameter. Used in conjunction with yield_target_pct to compute expected_good_die_qty.

Projected number of Known Good Dies (KGD) expected from this wafer start, calculated from authorized_wafer_qty, die per wafer count, and yield_target_pct. Used for order fulfillment planning and die bank replenishment forecasting.

Export control classification applicable to the product being fabricated under this authorization, per US EAR (Export Administration Regulations) and ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations). Determines whether export licenses are required before releasing wafers to certain fab sites or customers.

SAP PP production order or work order number generated in ERP when the wafer start authorization is released to manufacturing. Bridges the order management domain to the ERP manufacturing execution module for cost collection and WIP tracking.

Indicates whether this authorization is for a Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) run, where multiple customer designs share a single wafer to reduce NRE cost. MPW lots require special reticle set management and die allocation tracking.

Indicates whether this wafer start authorization is for a Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) run, such as a first-time tapeout, process qualification, or engineering lot, as opposed to a production volume order. NRE lots have distinct cost accounting and yield expectations.

System timestamp of the most recent modification to the wafer start authorization record. Fulfills the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED minimum category for TRANSACTION_HEADER. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and change tracking.

MES-assigned lot identifier for the wafer batch initiated under this authorization. Populated after the authorization is released to the fab and a lot is created in the MES (Camstar or SmartFactory). Links the authorization to the physical WIP lot in the fab.

Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or exceptions associated with this wafer start authorization. May include hot-lot justification, customer escalation notes, or fab-specific handling instructions.

Non-Recurring Engineering fee in US dollars associated with this wafer start authorization, applicable for first-time tapeouts, mask set fabrication, and engineering qualification lots. Populated when is_nre is True. Used for customer invoicing and cost recovery tracking.

Expected date by which wafer fabrication (FEOL through BEOL) is planned to be completed and wafers released for wafer test and probing. Drives downstream packaging and delivery commit date calculations.
Scheduled calendar date on which wafer fabrication is planned to begin at the fab site. Used for fab capacity planning, WIP (Work in Process) scheduling, and order commit date calculations.

Fabrication priority classification assigned to this wafer start authorization. Hot lots receive highest fab priority and cycle time targets; MPW (Multi-Project Wafer) lots share wafer capacity across multiple designs; NRE pilot lots are for non-recurring engineering qualification runs.

Target semiconductor process technology node for the wafer start (e.g., 5nm, 7nm, 14nm, 28nm, 65nm). Determines the process design kit (PDK) and fab line assignment. Critical for capacity planning and yield forecasting.

Internal fab process technology identifier (e.g., N5P, N7FF+, HVM28LP) that maps to a specific process flow within the fabrication facility. More granular than process_node; used for exact MES routing and recipe assignment.

Customer-committed or order-driven date by which finished product must ship. Used to back-calculate the wafer start date and validate that the planned_start_date supports on-time delivery. Key input to TTM (Time to Market) and delivery performance metrics.

Identifier of the operational system of record from which this wafer start authorization record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA PP, Camstar MES, Applied Materials SmartFactory, Salesforce CRM design-win). Supports data lineage and Silver Layer reconciliation.

Native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., SAP production order number, Camstar lot ID, Salesforce opportunity ID). Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and operational systems.
Diameter of the silicon wafer substrate in millimeters (e.g., 200mm, 300mm, 450mm). Determines equipment compatibility and fab line routing at the fabrication facility.
Standard cost per wafer in US dollars for the specified process node and fab site at the time of authorization. Used for cost of goods sold (COGS) estimation, margin analysis, and financial planning.

FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Wafer start authorizations are triggered by sales orders. This link is essential for demand-to-supply traceability and is explicitly mentioned in the WSA description.

Expected die yield percentage (good dies per wafer as a percentage of total die sites) for this wafer start, based on process node maturity and historical SPC data. Used to calculate expected good die output and validate order fulfillment feasibility.
Primary key for amendment

Reference to the customer account associated with the parent sales order. Included on the amendment for direct customer-level reporting, dispute resolution, and revenue recognition without requiring a join to the order header.

Reference to the Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) run affected by this amendment, if applicable. Populated when the amended order is associated with an MPW shuttle run, where quantity or cancellation changes affect shared wafer slot allocations.
FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Amendments modify existing orders. Each amendment must reference the order it modifies for audit trail integrity.

Indicates whether this amendment triggers a reallocation of constrained wafer or die inventory across customer orders. True when the quantity or delivery date change affects the allocation pool managed in SAP SD or the MES.

The name of the specific order field that was changed (e.g., 'requested_delivery_date', 'quantity', 'unit_price', 'product_code'). Together with original_value and revised_value, provides a complete field-level audit trail for each amendment.
The business date on which the amendment was formally raised and submitted by the requester. Used as the effective date for revenue recognition adjustments and backlog recalculation.

Externally visible, human-readable change order reference number assigned by SAP SD at the time the amendment is raised. Used in customer-facing communications and dispute resolution. Format: AMD-{YYYY}-{SEQUENCE}.

Current lifecycle state of the amendment record within the change order management workflow. Transitions from draft through approval to applied or rejected. Drives revenue recognition timing and backlog reporting.

Classifies the nature of the change applied to the sales order. Drives downstream workflow routing, revenue recognition treatment, and audit reporting. Allowed values: quantity_change, date_push_out, date_pull_in, cancellation, price_revision, product_substitution.

Date and time when the approved amendment was actually applied to the sales order in the system of record (SAP SD). Marks the point at which the order reflects the revised terms for fulfillment and revenue recognition.

The authorization tier required and applied for this amendment based on the revenue impact and amendment type. Higher-value or cancellation amendments require escalated approval levels per the delegation of authority matrix.

Date and time when the amendment was approved or rejected by the authorized approver. Distinct from amendment_date (submission) and applied_timestamp (system application). Critical for SLA measurement and audit compliance.
Name or user identifier of the authorized approver who accepted or rejected the amendment. Required for SOX-compliant change order audit trails and revenue recognition documentation.

Free-text explanation provided by the requester describing the business reason for the amendment (e.g., customer demand change, fab capacity constraint, end-market shift, design-win revision). Required for SOX audit trail and customer dispute resolution.

Standardized reason code for order cancellations, populated only when amendment_type is 'cancellation'. Examples include: CUST_REQUEST, CAPACITY_CONSTRAINT, EOL_PRODUCT, DESIGN_WIN_LOST, DUPLICATE_ORDER. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CUST_REQUEST|CAPACITY_CONSTRAINT|EOL_PRODUCT|DESIGN_WIN_LOST|DUPLICATE_ORDER|PRICE_DISPUTE|EXPORT_HOLD — promote to reference product]

Indicates whether the parent sales order is associated with a CHIPS Act-eligible program. Amendments to CHIPS Act orders may require additional compliance review and reporting under US CHIPS and Science Act requirements.
System timestamp when the amendment record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to price fields on this amendment (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, TWD). Required for multi-currency revenue recognition and financial reporting.

Indicates whether the amendment was initiated by the customer (True) or internally by the semiconductor company (False). Distinguishes customer-driven changes from internal supply chain or capacity-driven adjustments for analytics and dispute resolution.

The confirmed delivery date on the sales order line before the amendment. Populated for date_push_out and date_pull_in amendment types. Used to measure schedule change impact and customer on-time delivery performance.

The new confirmed delivery date after the amendment is applied. Populated for date_push_out and date_pull_in amendment types. Drives updated production scheduling, wafer start authorization, and customer delivery commitments.

Reference number of any associated customer dispute or claim raised in Salesforce CRM that prompted or resulted from this amendment. Links the amendment to the customer dispute resolution workflow for escalation tracking.

Indicates whether the amendment (particularly product substitution or destination change) requires re-review under export control regulations (EAR/ITAR). Ensures compliance with BIS/EAR and ITAR before the revised order is fulfilled.

The value of the amended field before the change was applied. Stored as STRING to accommodate heterogeneous amendment types (quantity, date, price, product code). Paired with revised_value and amended_field_name to form a complete before/after audit record.

Reference number of the Product Change Notification (PCN) that triggered this amendment, if applicable. Links the order amendment to a formal PCN issued under JEDEC JESD46 for product or process changes affecting customer orders.

The ordered quantity on the sales order line before the amendment was applied. Populated specifically for quantity_change amendment types. Enables direct numeric comparison and delta calculation for backlog management and wafer start authorization adjustments.

The ordered quantity on the sales order line after the amendment is applied. Populated for quantity_change amendment types. Used for backlog recalculation, wafer start authorization updates, and allocation management.
Unit of measure for the quantity fields on this amendment. Common semiconductor units include EA (each), KU (thousands of units), WAFER, DIE, and REEL for tape-and-reel packaged devices.

Name or user identifier of the individual (internal sales representative, customer service agent, or customer contact) who initiated the amendment request. Supports audit trail and dispute resolution.

The net change in order value resulting from this amendment, expressed in the order currency. Positive for upward revisions (pull-in, price increase), negative for reductions (push-out, cancellation, price decrease). Used directly in revenue recognition and backlog reporting.

The accounting period (YYYY-MM) in which the revenue impact of this amendment is recognized under ASC 606 / IFRS 15. Assigned by the finance team during amendment approval and used for period-close revenue reporting.

The new value of the amended field after the change is applied. Stored as STRING to accommodate heterogeneous amendment types. Paired with original_value and amended_field_name to form a complete before/after audit record.

The specific line item number within the parent sales order that this amendment affects. An amendment may target a single line (e.g., quantity change on one SKU) or the entire order header (e.g., cancellation). Null if the amendment applies to the full order.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this amendment record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking across SAP SD, Oracle Agile PLM, Salesforce CRM, and manual entry channels.

The product part number or material code of the replacement product, populated only when amendment_type is 'product_substitution'. Captures the new IC, SoC, or packaged device being substituted in place of the original ordered product.
FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Amendments modify a specific sales order. Required for change management audit trail.

The unit selling price on the sales order line before the price revision amendment. Populated for price_revision amendment types. Supports revenue recognition delta calculation and customer dispute resolution.
The new unit selling price after the price revision amendment is applied. Populated for price_revision amendment types. Used for revenue recognition recalculation and updated order value reporting.
System timestamp of the most recent update to this amendment record in the data platform. Supports incremental load detection and change data capture in the Databricks Silver layer.

Sequential version counter for amendments on the same sales order line, starting at 1. Enables tracking of iterative changes (e.g., multiple date push-outs on the same line) and supports optimistic concurrency control in the Silver layer.

Indicates whether this amendment requires a corresponding update to the Wafer Start Authorization (WSA) in the fab. True when quantity changes or cancellations affect in-flight WIP lots in the fabrication facility.
Unique surrogate identifier for each backlog position snapshot record in the order-to-cash lifecycle. Primary key for the backlog_position data product.
Reference to the customer account associated with this backlog position. Used for customer-level backlog aggregation, escalation prioritization, and revenue forecasting.
Reference to the parent sales order header to which this backlog position belongs. Links the backlog snapshot to the originating customer order.
FK to order.order_line.order_line_id — Backlog positions track unshipped quantities for specific order lines. Without this FK, backlog cannot be reconciled to orders.
Reference to the semiconductor product (IC, SoC, ASIC, FPGA, or discrete device) associated with this backlog position. Enables product-level backlog analysis and demand planning.

The quantity from available inventory or in-process production lots that has been formally allocated to fulfill this backlog position. May be less than committed_quantity in constrained supply scenarios.

Indicates the degree to which available inventory or production capacity has been allocated to fulfill this backlog position. Critical for backlog management and supply-demand balancing in constrained semiconductor supply environments.
Number of calendar days this order line has been in open backlog as of the snapshot date, calculated from order_entry_date. Used for customer escalation prioritization and aged backlog reporting.

Current workflow state of this backlog position. 'open' = unshipped committed order; 'committed' = confirmed with delivery date; 'at_risk' = delivery commitment in jeopardy; 'pushed_out' = delivery date deferred; 'cancelled' = order line cancelled; 'fulfilled' = shipped and closed.

The monetary value of the unshipped committed backlog quantity, calculated as committed_quantity multiplied by the net selling price. Used for revenue forecasting, quarterly guidance, and book-to-bill ratio reporting.
Quantity cancelled from the original order line. Tracks cancellation impact on backlog coverage and book-to-bill ratio reporting.
The unshipped quantity (in units) that remains committed in backlog for this order line as of the snapshot date. Core metric for backlog management and production planning.
The timestamp when this backlog position record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle management.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the backlog value and selling price are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, KRW, TWD). Required for multi-currency backlog reporting and FX exposure analysis.
The most recent confirmed delivery date committed to the customer, reflecting any push-outs or pull-ins from the original promise date. Used for revenue forecasting and quarterly close planning.
The customer's own part number or reference code for the ordered product. Required for customer-facing order confirmations and cross-referencing customer purchase orders with internal part numbers.
The customer's purchase order number referencing this backlog position. Required for customer reconciliation, invoice matching, and accounts receivable processing.

Indicates whether this backlog position is associated with a strategic design-win account tracked in Salesforce CRM. Design-win orders receive elevated fulfillment priority to protect long-term revenue relationships.

The end-market application segment for which the ordered semiconductor product is destined. Used for segment-level backlog analysis, demand forecasting, and investor guidance on market exposure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: computing|mobile|automotive|industrial|iot|communications|consumer|ai_datacenter — promote to reference product]
Indicates whether this order line is subject to export control regulations (EAR/ITAR). When true, shipment requires validated export license or license exception before fulfillment.
The export license number issued by the Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) or State Department authorizing shipment of controlled semiconductor products. Required when export_control_flag is true.

Code indicating the reason this backlog position is on hold and cannot be shipped. Examples include credit hold, export license pending, quality hold, or customer-requested hold. Null when no hold is active. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_hold|export_hold|quality_hold|customer_hold|compliance_hold|capacity_hold — promote to reference product]

International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between seller and buyer for this order line. Impacts revenue recognition timing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The timestamp when this backlog position record was most recently modified, reflecting the latest change to any field such as commit date revision, quantity adjustment, or status change.
The agreed net unit selling price for this order line after all applicable discounts and price adjustments. Used to compute backlog value and support revenue recognition under ASC 606.
The date on which the customer order was originally entered into the order management system. Used to calculate backlog aging and time-in-backlog metrics.

Classification of the order type driving this backlog position. Distinguishes standard production orders from Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) orders, die bank orders, wafer start authorizations, sample orders, Return Material Authorizations (RMA), and consignment orders. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|mpw|die_bank|wafer_start|sample|rma|consignment — promote to reference product]
The total quantity originally ordered by the customer on this order line. Used to calculate fulfillment percentage and track cancellation or reduction impacts on backlog.
The initial delivery date committed to the customer at the time of order entry. Baseline for measuring push-outs and delivery performance against original commitment.
The manufacturer's part number (MPN) of the semiconductor product ordered. Used for product identification, cross-referencing with PLM and ERP systems, and customer-facing order documentation.

Numeric priority ranking assigned to this backlog position for allocation and fulfillment sequencing. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used during supply-constrained periods to prioritize strategic customers and design-win accounts.

Number of calendar days the current_commit_date has been deferred beyond the original_promise_date. Positive value indicates a push-out (delay); negative value indicates a pull-in (acceleration). Key metric for delivery performance and customer satisfaction.

Standardized reason code explaining why the delivery commitment was pushed out or pulled in. Examples include wafer yield issue, capacity constraint, material shortage, customer request, or logistics delay. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: yield_issue|capacity_constraint|material_shortage|customer_request|logistics_delay|design_change|export_hold — promote to reference product]
The delivery date originally requested by the customer on the purchase order. May differ from the original promise date if the committed date was negotiated.

Indicates whether this backlog position meets the criteria for revenue recognition upon shipment under ASC 606 / IFRS 15. False indicates revenue deferral conditions apply (e.g., consignment, bill-and-hold arrangements).
Geographic sales region associated with this backlog position (e.g., Americas, EMEA, Japan, Greater China, Rest of APAC). Used for regional backlog reporting and revenue forecasting.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country for shipment. Used for export control screening, logistics routing, and regional revenue reporting.
Cumulative quantity already shipped against this order line as of the snapshot date. Together with committed_quantity, reconciles to the original order quantity.
The calendar date on which this point-in-time backlog position snapshot was captured. Enables time-series backlog trending, quarterly close reporting, and book-to-bill ratio calculation.

The unit in which quantities are expressed for this backlog position. EA = each (individual units), KU = thousands of units, WAFER = full wafer, DIE = individual die, REEL = tape-and-reel packaging unit, TRAY = tray packaging unit.

The fabrication lot number of the wafer batch assigned to fulfill this backlog position. Links the backlog to the specific Work in Process (WIP) lot in the fab for production tracking and yield impact analysis.
Unique surrogate identifier for the blanket purchase agreement record in the Databricks Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the customer (OEM, automotive Tier-1, or distributor) who has entered into this blanket purchase agreement.

Reference to the design win record in Salesforce CRM that originated this blanket order. Links the long-term supply commitment back to the customer design-in event and program qualification milestone.
Reference to the account manager or field sales representative who owns the customer relationship and negotiated this blanket agreement.
Reference to the SAP sales organization responsible for managing and fulfilling this blanket order agreement.

The contractually agreed price per unit for the product under this blanket order. May be a single fixed price or the base tier price when pricing tiers are defined. Expressed in the contract currency.

Classification of the blanket order agreement type. Scheduling agreements define delivery cadence; quantity contracts commit to total volume; value contracts commit to total spend; MPW (Multi-Project Wafer) blankets cover shuttle run commitments; distributor frame agreements govern stocking distributor relationships.

Numeric priority ranking (lower value = higher priority) used during constrained supply allocation to determine which blanket orders receive preferential wafer starts and finished goods inventory. Automotive and safety-critical programs typically receive highest priority.

The timestamp when the blanket order agreement was formally approved by the authorized signatory (e.g., VP of Sales or Finance Controller) and transitioned from draft to active status. Required for SOX revenue recognition controls.

Indicates whether this blanket order agreement is configured to automatically renew for an additional contract period upon expiry if neither party issues a termination notice within the agreed notice window.

Externally visible alphanumeric identifier for the blanket purchase agreement, used in customer-facing communications, SAP SD scheduling agreements, and ERP cross-references. Follows the BO- prefix convention.
The agreed cadence at which the customer issues individual release orders (call-offs) against this blanket agreement. Drives wafer start planning, fab scheduling, and inventory build strategy.

Indicates whether the product or program covered by this blanket order qualifies for US CHIPS and Science Act incentives or domestic content requirements. Relevant for US government-funded programs and domestic semiconductor supply chain initiatives.

The total quantity of units (dies, packaged ICs, or wafers) the customer has committed to purchase over the full contract period. This is the binding volume commitment underpinning the blanket agreement.

Total duration of the blanket order agreement expressed in calendar months. Standard semiconductor blanket agreements run 12 or 24 months; automotive long-term supply agreements may extend to 36 months.

The timestamp when this blanket order record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA SD). Represents the business event of agreement entry, not the ETL load time. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this blanket order (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, KRW, TWD). Semiconductor contracts are predominantly denominated in USD.

The customer's own purchase contract or frame agreement reference number as provided on the customer's procurement documentation. Used for cross-referencing with the customer's ERP or procurement system.
The customer's own part number or device designation for the product covered by this blanket agreement. Required for cross-reference on customer purchase orders and shipping documents.

The sales channel through which this blanket order is fulfilled. Direct: sold directly to end customer; Distributor: fulfilled via authorized stocking distributor; Rep Firm: managed through manufacturer's representative; OEM: original equipment manufacturer direct program; EMS: electronics manufacturing services partner.

The date on which the blanket order agreement becomes legally binding and call-off releases may begin. Marks the start of the contract period, typically aligned with the customer's fiscal year or program start.

The primary end-market application segment for the product covered by this blanket order. Used for revenue segmentation, demand planning, and strategic reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: automotive|mobile|computing|industrial|iot|ai_datacenter|aerospace_defense|consumer|networking|medical — promote to reference product]

The date on which the blanket order agreement expires and no further call-off releases may be issued. Typically 12 to 24 months from the effective date for semiconductor OEM and automotive Tier-1 agreements.

Export Administration Regulations (EAR) Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) for the product covered by this blanket order. Determines whether an export license is required for shipment to the customer's country. Critical for semiconductor products subject to BIS/EAR controls.

Indicates whether an export license is required to ship product under this blanket order to the customer's destination country based on the ECCN and end-use classification. Triggers compliance review workflow when True.

International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between Semiconductors and the customer for shipments under this blanket agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

business_glossary_term: International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) Controlled Flag
description: Indicates whether the product or technology covered by this blanket order is subject to International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) controls. When True, additional compliance approvals and end-use certificates are required before shipment.
The timestamp of the most recent update to this blanket order record in the source system. Tracks amendments to pricing, quantities, dates, or terms. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

The date of the most recent call-off release order issued against this blanket agreement. Used to monitor customer ordering activity, identify dormant blankets, and trigger account management follow-up.

The agreed manufacturing and delivery lead time in weeks from call-off release order placement to customer delivery. Encompasses wafer fab cycle time, assembly and test (OSAT), and logistics. Critical for wafer start authorization scheduling.

The minimum quantity per individual call-off release order as agreed in the blanket contract. Enforces production lot efficiency and wafer start economics. Expressed in the same unit of measure as committed_quantity.
The percentage of the committed quantity or blanket value that is subject to NCNR terms. A value of 100 means the entire commitment is NCNR; partial NCNR coverage is common in distributor agreements.

Indicates whether the blanket order is subject to Non-Cancellable Non-Returnable (NCNR) terms. When True, the customer cannot cancel or return product once a release order is issued. Standard for automotive and high-volume OEM semiconductor supply agreements.

Current lifecycle state of the blanket order agreement. Draft: under negotiation; Active: binding and open for call-offs; On Hold: temporarily suspended; Partially Released: some call-off orders issued; Fully Released: all committed quantity has been called off; Closed: contract period ended; Cancelled: terminated before expiry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|active|on_hold|partially_released|fully_released|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

SAP payment terms code defining the invoice due date, early payment discount conditions, and payment schedule applicable to release orders issued under this blanket agreement (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30).

Defines the pricing model applied to this blanket order. Fixed: single price for all quantities; Volume Tiered: unit price decreases at defined quantity thresholds; Time Tiered: price adjusts at defined calendar intervals; Milestone Tiered: price adjusts upon reaching program milestones such as design-in or production ramp.
The Semiconductors internal part number (device identifier) for the IC, SoC, ASIC, or FPGA product covered by this blanket order. Aligns with the product master in SAP MM and Oracle Agile PLM.

Unit of measure for the committed and released quantities. EA = each (packaged unit); WFR = wafer; DIE = bare die; REEL = tape-and-reel packaging unit; TRAY = tray packaging unit. Aligns with SAP MM unit of measure configuration.

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the product and its packaging materials comply with EU REACH Regulation (EC No 1907/2006) requirements for chemical substance registration and restriction. Required for EU market access.
FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Individual sales orders (release orders) are created against blanket orders. The sales order must reference its parent blanket for volume tracking.

Cumulative quantity that has been called off against this blanket order through individual release orders to date. Used to track consumption of the committed volume and compute remaining open quantity.
The number of calendar days prior to the expiry date by which either party must provide written notice to prevent automatic renewal or to initiate renegotiation of the blanket agreement terms.

business_glossary_term: Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Compliant Flag
description: Indicates whether the product shipped under this blanket order is compliant with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting hazardous substances in electronic equipment. Mandatory for EU market shipments.

FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Individual sales orders are call-offs against blanket orders. This link is critical for blanket quantity consumption tracking and volume commitment management.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary customer delivery destination under this blanket agreement. Used for export control classification, logistics routing, and trade compliance screening.

The total monetary value of the blanket order agreement, calculated as committed_quantity multiplied by agreed_unit_price (or sum of tier values). Represents the maximum revenue commitment from this customer for the contract period. Used for revenue forecasting and SOX financial reporting.
Primary key for order
Reference to the customer who placed the sales order. Links to the customer master data product.

Foreign key linking to order.blanket_order. Business justification: An order may be covered by a blanket purchase agreement; adding FK from order to blanket_order captures this relationship without creating a cycle.
Foreign key linking to sales.channel_partner. Business justification: Orders may be placed via channel partners; the FK enables partner‑attributed revenue and commission reporting.

Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Order Management requires a primary sales contact for each order; the Order Confirmation Report pulls contact details, making this link essential.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Orders are charged to a cost center for expense tracking and budgeting in the finance system.
Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Export compliance reporting requires linking each order to its ECCN classification for customs documentation.
Reference to the sales representative or account manager responsible for managing this order and the customer relationship.

Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Export License Management: each sales order must reference the approved export license, replacing the free‑text export_license_number.

Reference to the Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) run associated with this order. Applicable only for MPW order types where multiple customer designs share a single wafer to reduce Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) costs.
Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Order creation originates from a closed‑won opportunity; linking enables opportunity‑to‑order conversion reporting.
Reference to the SAP sales organization responsible for this order, defining the selling entity, pricing, and legal entity context.

Foreign key linking to invoice.payment_term. Business justification: Required for order processing: each order references a centrally defined payment term for financial reporting and compliance with contract terms.

Foreign key linking to customer.price_agreement. Business justification: Pricing Governance applies a specific price agreement to an order; the Pricing Audit Report and revenue recognition rely on this relationship.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit‑center reporting requires each sales order to be linked to the responsible profit center.
Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Orders are generated from accepted quotes; the link supports quote‑to‑order tracking and pricing audit.

Foreign key linking to research.program. Business justification: Program‑driven volume orders must be associated with the research program for financial tracking, budget compliance, and KPI dashboards.
Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Custom design order fulfillment tracks the originating research project for cost allocation and project KPI reporting.
Foreign key linking to compliance.restricted_party_screening. Business justification: Trade compliance hold process records the screening result for the customer/account on the order.
Reference to the Salesforce CRM design-win opportunity that originated this sales order. Links the order to the customer design-in engagement, enabling design-win to revenue conversion tracking.

Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Shipping Execution process stores the exact customer address for each order; the Shipping Manifest and compliance checks depend on this link.
The actual date on which the ordered products were delivered to the customer or ship-to location. Used for on-time delivery (OTD) performance analysis and customer satisfaction reporting.

Current inventory or production capacity allocation status of the order. Indicates whether sufficient die, wafer, or finished goods inventory has been reserved to fulfill the order. Critical for backlog management and supply-demand balancing.

Indicates whether this order is currently included in the active order backlog. True when the order is open and not yet fully shipped or invoiced. Used for backlog valuation, revenue forecasting, and capacity planning.

Business reason code or description for order cancellation. Populated only when order_status is CANCELLED. Used for lost revenue analysis, demand forecasting accuracy, and customer relationship management.
Indicates whether this order is associated with products or programs eligible for US CHIPS and Science Act incentives or reporting requirements. Used for government reporting and subsidy tracking.

The delivery date confirmed by the semiconductor manufacturer based on production capacity, wafer fab scheduling, and logistics. May differ from the requested delivery date. Used for backlog management and customer commitment tracking.

Timestamp when the sales order record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA SD). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and order entry performance measurement. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.ERDAT + VBAK.ERZET.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the order transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, TWD, KRW). Defines the currency in which order values, pricing, and invoicing are denominated. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.WAERK.

The purchase order number provided by the customer at the time of order placement. Used for cross-referencing with the customer's procurement system and required for invoice matching. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.BSTNK.
The sales channel through which the order was placed. Determines pricing tiers, commission structures, and revenue reporting by channel. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.VTWEG.

The end-market application segment for which the ordered semiconductor products are destined. Used for market segmentation analysis, revenue reporting by vertical, and strategic planning. Total gross value of the sales order before application of discounts, taxes, or surcharges, expressed in the order currency. Represents the sum of all line item gross values. Used for revenue forecasting and backlog valuation.

The named place or port associated with the Incoterms code, specifying where risk and cost transfer between buyer and seller (e.g., 'Shanghai Port', 'Customer Dock'). Corresponds to SAP VBAK.INCO2.

Indicates whether any products in this order are subject to ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations) controls. When True, additional export authorization and end-use certification requirements apply before shipment.

Net value of the sales order after application of all discounts and pricing conditions but before tax. Represents the contractually agreed revenue amount. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.NETWR. Used for revenue recognition and financial reporting.

Free-text notes or special instructions associated with the sales order, capturing customer-specific requirements, handling instructions, or internal processing notes. Corresponds to SAP order header text.

Current lifecycle status of the sales order within the order-to-cash process. Drives workflow routing, backlog reporting, and revenue recognition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DRAFT|OPEN|IN_FULFILLMENT|SHIPPED|DELIVERED|CLOSED|CANCELLED|ON_HOLD — promote to reference product]

SAP payment terms key defining the payment due date, early payment discount conditions, and cash discount percentages applicable to this order. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.ZTERM (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10NET30).

Business priority level assigned to the order, influencing production scheduling, wafer start queue position, and allocation decisions. Critical orders may trigger expedite fees or override standard allocation rules.
