The date by which the customer has requested delivery of the ordered products. Used for delivery scheduling, wafer start planning, and on-time delivery (OTD) performance measurement. Corresponds to SAP VBAK.VDATU.

Indicates whether the products ordered comply with the EU RoHS (Restriction of Hazardous Substances) Directive, restricting the use of specific hazardous materials in electronic equipment. Required for EU market access and customer compliance declarations.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country for shipment. Critical for export compliance screening (EAR/ITAR/BIS), trade sanctions checks, and logistics routing. Corresponds to SAP ship-to party country.

Externally visible sales order number as assigned by SAP S/4HANA SD module. Used in all customer-facing communications, shipping documents, and invoices. Corresponds to the VBELN field in SAP VBAK table.
The channel or system through which the customer order was received and entered. Used for order entry efficiency analysis and digital transformation tracking.
Total tax amount applicable to the sales order, including VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes, expressed in the order currency. Required for tax reporting and financial compliance.

Indicates whether this order has triggered a wafer start authorization in the fabrication facility (FAB). When True, the MES (Manufacturing Execution System) has been instructed to initiate wafer lot processing for this order.
Unique surrogate identifier for each hold record placed on a sales order or order line within the SAP S/4HANA SD order block management module. Primary key for the order.hold data product.

Reference to the quality lot or wafer lot under disposition that triggered a QUALITY hold on the order. Links to the quality domain for full traceability between order holds and quality management workflows. Null for non-quality holds.

Reference to the Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) run associated with the held order, if applicable. Enables traceability between order holds and MPW shuttle schedules, which have fixed tapeout and delivery dates that may be impacted by hold duration.

Reference to the specific order line item on which this hold is placed. Null if the hold applies to the entire sales order header rather than a specific line. Enables line-level hold granularity for partial shipment scenarios.

Reference to the employee or team responsible for managing and resolving this hold. Typically the credit analyst, export compliance officer, quality engineer, or customer service representative who initiated or owns the hold. Links to the workforce domain for accountability tracking.
Reference to the parent sales order on which this hold has been placed. Links the hold record to the order.order data product for full order-to-cash lifecycle traceability.
Reference to the senior manager or compliance officer to whom the hold has been escalated. Null if not escalated. Enables accountability tracking for escalated holds and management reporting.
Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_compliance_hold. Business justification: Order holds can be triggered by a trade compliance hold; linking enables root‑cause analysis.

business_glossary_term: CHIPS Act Compliance Review Required Flag
description: Indicates whether this hold requires a CHIPS Act compliance review, particularly for orders involving customers or end-uses that may conflict with CHIPS Act guardrail provisions restricting expansion of semiconductor manufacturing capacity in countries of concern.

Date and time when this hold record was first created in the data platform. Represents the audit trail creation timestamp for the Silver layer record, distinct from the hold_date which captures the business event time.

The customer credit exposure amount in the order currency at the time the credit hold was placed. Applicable only for CREDIT hold type. Represents the outstanding accounts receivable balance plus open order value that triggered the credit block in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR.

The approved credit limit for the customer at the time the credit hold was placed. Applicable only for CREDIT hold type. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR credit management. Used to calculate credit utilization and assess hold severity.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the credit exposure and credit limit amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency semiconductor customer accounts.

Customer-provided reference number, purchase order amendment number, or communication reference that authorized or requested the hold. Applicable for CUSTOMER_REQUEST hold type. Enables traceability between the hold and the customer's formal request documentation in Salesforce CRM.

Indicates whether this hold has blocked shipment from the die bank inventory associated with the sales order. True = die bank order is impacted; False = die bank order is not affected. Used for die bank inventory management and allocation planning.

The Defective Parts Per Million (DPPM) threshold that was exceeded and triggered the quality hold. Captures the specific quality metric breach driving the hold for QUALITY hold types. Used for quality performance analysis and customer quality reporting.

Date and time when the hold was escalated to senior management or a compliance officer. Null if the hold has not been escalated. Used to measure escalation response time and management intervention frequency.

Indicates whether this hold has been escalated to senior management or a compliance officer due to complexity, duration, or business impact. True = escalated; False = not escalated. Triggers escalation workflow notifications in SAP S/4HANA.

Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) or USML category of the product on the held order that triggered the export compliance review. Captures the specific classification driving the hold for EXPORT_COMPLIANCE hold types. Aligns with EAR/BIS and ITAR regulatory requirements.

The export license or authorization number being verified or awaited when the hold type is EXPORT_COMPLIANCE. References the specific BIS/EAR license, ITAR authorization, or equivalent export control document required before shipment can proceed. Null for non-export-compliance holds.

Date and time when the hold was placed on the sales order or order line. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the hold lifecycle. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD order block creation timestamp.

Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the hold record as assigned by SAP S/4HANA SD order block management. Used in customer communications, credit team workflows, and export compliance tracking.

Current lifecycle state of the hold record. ACTIVE = hold is in effect and order processing is blocked; RELEASED = hold has been resolved and order processing may resume; ESCALATED = hold has been escalated to senior management or compliance officer; CANCELLED = hold was voided without formal release (e.g., duplicate entry).

Classification of the hold category that determines the workflow and approval path required for release. CREDIT = credit limit exceeded or payment overdue; EXPORT_COMPLIANCE = pending export license or ITAR/EAR review; QUALITY = quality disposition pending; CUSTOMER_REQUEST = customer-initiated pause; ALLOCATION = insufficient die bank or wafer inventory; REGULATORY = RoHS/REACH/CHIPS Act compliance verification pending.

business_glossary_term: International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) Controlled Flag
description: Indicates whether the hold is related to International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) controlled technology or products. True = ITAR-controlled item requiring State Department authorization; False = not ITAR-controlled. Drives export compliance workflow routing and documentation requirements.

Net value of the sales order or order line blocked by this hold, expressed in the order currency. Represents the revenue at risk due to the hold and is used for business impact assessment, management reporting, and prioritization of hold resolution efforts.

Business department responsible for resolving the hold. Drives workflow routing and SLA assignment. CREDIT_MGMT = credit and collections team; EXPORT_COMPLIANCE = trade compliance and ITAR/EAR team; QUALITY = quality assurance and reliability team; CUSTOMER_SERVICE = order management team; SUPPLY_CHAIN = allocation and inventory team; FINANCE = accounts receivable.

Full name of the employee or team responsible for managing and resolving this hold at the time it was placed. Denormalized for operational reporting and audit trail purposes without requiring a join to the workforce domain.

Current disposition decision status for the quality lot associated with a QUALITY hold. PENDING = disposition not yet determined; USE_AS_IS = lot approved for shipment as-is; REWORK = lot requires rework before shipment; SCRAP = lot scrapped, order must be re-sourced; RETURN_TO_SUPPLIER = material returned to supplier. Null for non-quality holds.

Detailed narrative explanation of why the hold was placed on the order. Captures the specific business condition triggering the block, such as credit limit exceeded amount, export license number pending, quality lot disposition reference, or customer-requested delivery pause reason. Free-text field sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD order block reason text.

Standardized SAP S/4HANA SD order block reason code (e.g., 01 = Credit Block, 02 = Export Block, 03 = Quality Block) used for systematic reporting and workflow routing. Complements the free-text hold_reason field with a machine-readable classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|02|03|04|05|06|07|08|09|10 — promote to reference product]
Full name of the employee who approved the release of the hold at the time of release. Denormalized for audit trail and compliance reporting without requiring a join to the workforce domain.

Date and time when the hold was formally released and order processing was authorized to resume. Null if the hold is still active. Used to calculate hold duration and measure order management cycle time performance.

Narrative explanation of the resolution that enabled the hold to be released. Captures the specific action taken, such as credit limit increase approved, export license number obtained, quality lot disposition completed, or customer confirmation received. Free-text field for audit and compliance documentation.

The date by which the hold is expected or requested to be released, as agreed with the customer or set by the hold owner. Used for proactive hold management, customer commitment tracking, and delivery schedule impact assessment.

Extended free-text notes documenting the full resolution process, including any corrective actions taken, approvals obtained, reference documents reviewed, and follow-up actions required. Supports post-hold analysis and process improvement initiatives.

Indicates whether the hold resolution exceeded the defined SLA target hours. True = SLA was breached; False = resolved within SLA. Used for order management performance reporting and customer escalation management.

business_glossary_term: Hold Resolution Service Level Agreement (SLA) Target Hours
description: Target number of business hours within which the hold must be resolved per the order management SLA policy. Varies by hold type: credit holds typically 24 hours, export compliance holds 72 hours, quality holds 48 hours. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and customer commitment management.

The native hold or order block identifier as recorded in the originating operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA SD order block number). Enables reconciliation between the Silver layer data product and the source system of record for data lineage and audit purposes.

Date and time when this hold record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks the most recent update to any hold attribute, supporting change data capture, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing.

Indicates whether this hold has blocked or delayed a wafer start authorization (WSA) associated with the sales order. True = wafer start is impacted; False = wafer start is not affected. Critical for fab capacity planning and WIP management in semiconductor manufacturing.
Unique surrogate identifier for each order line item within the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for the order_line data product.
Reference to the customer placing the order. Used for line-level customer attribution in multi-ship-to or multi-sold-to scenarios. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD KUNNR (Customer Number).

Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: REQUIRED: Order fulfillment report needs to know which wafer lot supplies each order line; linking enables traceability from line to physical wafer lot.

Foreign key linking to packaging.package_type. Business justification: Needed for Packaging Planning; order line specifies requested package type, linking to package_type enables yield, cost, and compliance analysis per packaging family.

Reference to the parent sales order header to which this line item belongs. Establishes the header-to-line relationship in the order-to-cash lifecycle. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD VBELN (Sales Document Number).
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Manufacturing assigns a process engineer to each order line for fab planning and yield optimization.
Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: Each order line is derived from a quote line; needed for line‑level margin and fulfillment analysis.
Foreign key linking to compliance.reach_svhc_declaration. Business justification: REACH SVHC Declaration is required per SKU on an order line to certify hazardous substance compliance.
Foreign key linking to research.project. Business justification: Line‑item level tracking of research project origin enables precise billing, milestone status, and project performance analysis.

Reference to the semiconductor product (IC, SoC, ASIC, FPGA, or discrete device) being ordered on this line. Links to the product master for SKU-level configuration details including package type, speed grade, and temperature range.
SSOT reference to canonical invoice.invoice_line (de-duplicates cross-domain concept).
Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Primary supplier for the SKU on each order line, used in procurement planning and supplier performance reporting.

Foreign key linking to test.test_program. Business justification: Required for Test Program Assignment per SKU during order fulfillment; ensures each order line selects the correct test program for quality validation.
Foreign key linking to equipment.tool_chamber. Business justification: CHAMBER_TRACEABILITY: Link order line to specific tool chamber used, enabling yield analysis and compliance reporting.

The date on which the semiconductor units for this order line were physically shipped from the warehouse or OSAT facility. Populated upon goods issue posting in SAP S/4HANA. Used for on-time delivery (OTD) performance measurement.

Classification of the supply allocation method applied to this order line. Standard = normal backlog allocation; Priority = customer-priority or design-win protected allocation; Strategic = long-term agreement allocation; Spot = spot market fulfillment; Buffer = safety stock draw.

Reason code for cancellation of this order line when line_status is 'cancelled'. Supports backlog analysis, demand planning accuracy, and customer relationship management. Populated only for cancelled lines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|supply_constraint|end_of_life|duplicate|pricing_dispute|export_hold|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The quantity of semiconductor units confirmed by supply chain for delivery on this order line, reflecting available inventory, wafer start authorizations, and die bank allocations. May differ from ordered quantity due to allocation constraints.

The date confirmed by supply chain and manufacturing for shipment of this order line, based on available inventory, die bank status, and OSAT packaging capacity. May differ from requested ship date due to supply constraints.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the semiconductor product was manufactured (FAB location). Required for customs declarations, trade compliance, tariff classification, and CHIPS Act domestic content reporting.

System timestamp when this order line record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking. Conforms to ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction currency of this order line (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, KRW, TWD). Semiconductor industry commonly transacts in USD but supports multi-currency for global customers.

The customer's own internal part number or cross-reference number for the ordered semiconductor product. Critical for design-win tracking, customer portal integration, and order acknowledgment matching. Sourced from Salesforce CRM design-win records.
The business date on which this order line was entered into the SAP S/4HANA SD system. Represents the commercial event date for backlog aging calculations and order-to-cash cycle time measurement.

Indicates whether this order line is to be fulfilled from the die bank (inventory of known good dies (KGD) awaiting packaging) rather than from finished goods or new wafer starts. True = die bank fulfillment; False = standard finished goods or new production.

Export Administration Regulations (EAR) Export Control Classification Number (ECCN) for the semiconductor product on this order line (e.g., 3A001 for advanced ICs). Required for export compliance screening, license determination, and ITAR/EAR regulatory reporting.

International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between the semiconductor company and the customer for this order line (e.g., FOB, DDP, EXW). Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD INCO1. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

SAP SD item category classification for this order line, indicating the business nature of the line (e.g., standard product sale, consignment fill-up, customer return, engineering sample, NRE charge, MPW service, evaluation unit). Drives pricing, delivery, and billing behavior. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|consignment|returns|sample|nre|mpw|evaluation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Sequential position number of this line item within the parent sales order. Used for ordering, display, and reference in customer-facing documents. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD POSNR (Item Number of the SD Document).

Current workflow status of this order line item within the order-to-cash lifecycle. Drives backlog management, allocation decisions, and revenue recognition. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD overall delivery status at item level.

The manufacturing lot number assigned to the production batch fulfilling this order line. Enables traceability from customer order back to wafer fabrication lot, die bank lot, and OSAT packaging lot. Sourced from Camstar MES lot tracking.

SAP material number (MATNR) identifying the specific semiconductor product SKU ordered on this line. Includes full part number with package, speed grade, and temperature range encoded per internal part numbering convention.

Indicates whether this order line is associated with a Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) run, where multiple customer designs share a single wafer to reduce NRE costs. True = MPW order; False = dedicated production lot order.

Total net value of this order line calculated as confirmed quantity multiplied by unit price in the transaction currency. Used for backlog reporting, revenue forecasting, and SOX-compliant revenue recognition. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD NETWR (Net Value of the Order Item).

The quantity of semiconductor units (dies, packaged ICs, wafers, or reels) requested by the customer on this order line. Expressed in the sales unit of measure (e.g., pieces, wafers, reels). Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD KWMENG (Cumulative Order Quantity).

Indicates whether the customer permits partial shipments against this order line. True = partial shipments accepted; False = complete order line quantity must ship together. Drives delivery scheduling and backlog management decisions.

The silicon revision or stepping of the ordered semiconductor product (e.g., A0, B1, C2). Critical for tracking product change notifications (PCNs), errata management, and ensuring customers receive the correct silicon revision per their qualification.

Indicates whether the semiconductor product on this order line is compliant with the EU REACH Regulation (Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals). Required for EU chemical safety compliance and customer material declarations.
The date by which the customer requires receipt of the semiconductor units at their facility. Distinct from requested ship date; accounts for transit time. Used for customer SLA compliance tracking.

The date on which the customer has requested shipment of this order line. Drives production scheduling, wafer start authorization timelines, and OSAT packaging commitments. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD WUNSK (Customer's Requested Delivery Date at Item Level).

Indicates whether the semiconductor product on this order line is compliant with the EU Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Directive, restricting use of lead, mercury, cadmium, and other hazardous materials. Required for EU market access.

business_glossary_term: SAP Sales Order Line Item Number
description: The native SAP S/4HANA SD six-digit item number (POSNR) for this order line, used for traceability back to the system of record and for EDI/customer portal reconciliation.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination country of this order line shipment. Used for export control screening, customs documentation, tariff determination, and trade compliance reporting.

Cumulative quantity of semiconductor units actually shipped against this order line across all partial shipments. Used for backlog calculation and revenue recognition. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD delivery documents.

Code indicating special handling, storage, or shipping requirements for this order line (e.g., ESD-sensitive, moisture-sensitive level (MSL), controlled humidity, ITAR-controlled, hazmat). Drives warehouse and logistics instructions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ESD|MSL1|MSL2|MSL3|ITAR|HAZMAT|CONTROLLED_TEMP|FRAGILE — promote to reference product]

The speed or performance grade of the ordered semiconductor product, indicating the maximum operating frequency or timing specification (e.g., -1, -2, -3 for FPGAs; 3200MHz, 4800MHz for memory). Differentiates SKUs within the same product family.

Operating temperature range classification for the ordered semiconductor product. Commercial (0°C to 70°C), Industrial (-40°C to 85°C), Automotive (-40°C to 125°C, AEC-Q100), Military (-55°C to 125°C). Determines qualification standard and pricing tier.

FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Fundamental header-to-line relationship. Every order line must belong to exactly one sales order. This is the most critical FK in the domain — without it, order lines are orphaned.

Sales unit of measure for the ordered semiconductor product. EA = Each (individual packaged IC), WFR = Wafer, REEL = Tape-and-reel packaging, TRAY = JEDEC tray, TUBE = Tube packaging. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD VRKME (Sales Unit).

Agreed net unit selling price for the semiconductor product on this order line, expressed in the transaction currency. Reflects negotiated pricing, volume discounts, and design-win pricing agreements. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA SD NETPR (Net Price).

System timestamp of the most recent update to this order line record in the data platform. Supports change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.

Indicates whether a wafer start authorization (WSA) has been issued to the fabrication facility (FAB) to initiate production of wafers for fulfilling this order line. True = WSA issued; False = pending or not required (e.g., fulfilled from die bank or finished goods inventory).
Primary key for status_history
Reference to the die bank or wafer allocation record when the status transitions to allocated. Captures which inventory allocation was committed to fulfill this order.

The user login ID or system service account that initiated the status transition. Supports accountability tracking and audit compliance. May be a human user (SAP logon ID) or an automated system process (e.g., MES_AUTO, ERP_BATCH).

Reference to the wafer fabrication lot associated with this order status event. Populated when the status transition is triggered by a fab event such as wafer_started or in_fab. Links order lifecycle to WIP (Work in Process) tracking.

business_glossary_term: Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test (OSAT) Partner ID
description: Reference to the OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) partner responsible for packaging and final test of the order. Populated when the order transitions to packaging or test phases. Supports OSAT-level delivery tracking.
FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Status history records track transitions for a specific order. Must reference the parent order.
Reference to the outbound shipment record when the status transitions to shipped. Links the order status history to the physical delivery event for shipment tracking and delivery confirmation.

Classifies the reason for order cancellation when new_status is cancelled. Values: customer_request (customer-initiated), eol (End of Life product discontinuation), capacity (fab capacity unavailable), yield_fail (unacceptable yield), export_block (export compliance hold), duplicate (duplicate order entry). Null for non-cancellation transitions.

Sequential integer representing the ordinal position of this status transition within the complete history of the sales order line. Sequence 1 is the initial entry event. Enables ordered reconstruction of the full order lifecycle.

The internally confirmed delivery date at the time of this status transition. May differ from requested_delivery_date due to fab capacity, yield, or supply chain constraints. Tracks delivery commitment evolution across the order lifecycle.

The timestamp when this status history record was first written to the data platform (Silver layer ingestion time). Distinct from transition_timestamp which records the business event time. Supports data lineage and audit trail completeness.

Indicates whether this order line is fulfilled from die bank inventory (pre-fabricated, unpackaged dies held in inventory) rather than a new wafer start. Die bank orders have a compressed status lifecycle bypassing wafer_started and in_fab states.

The number of hours the order line spent in the prior_status before transitioning to new_status. Calculated at ingestion time from the difference between this transition_timestamp and the previous record's transition_timestamp. Supports cycle time and SLA analytics.

Indicates whether this status transition was associated with a customer escalation event. When True, the order was under active escalation management at the time of the transition. Supports escalation tracking and priority queue analytics.

Priority level of the customer escalation active at the time of this status transition. P1 is highest urgency (e.g., automotive line-down risk), P4 is lowest. Populated only when escalation_flag is True.

Indicates whether this status transition triggered or was subject to an export control review under EAR or ITAR regulations. When True, the order required compliance screening before proceeding to the next status.

Standardized hold code applied to the order at the time of this status transition (e.g., CREDIT_HOLD, EXPORT_REVIEW, YIELD_HOLD, CAPACITY_HOLD). Null when no hold is active. Critical for backlog management and compliance review workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]

Indicates whether this record represents the most recent (current) status of the order line. True for the latest status transition record; False for all historical records. Enables efficient current-state queries without window functions.

Indicates whether the order line is associated with a Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) run. MPW orders share wafer capacity across multiple customers and have distinct status transition rules compared to dedicated production orders.

The order status assigned as a result of this transition event. Represents the current state after the change. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entered|confirmed|allocated|wafer_started|in_fab|shipped|invoiced|cancelled — promote to reference product]

The specific line item number within the sales order to which this status transition applies. A single sales order may have multiple lines (e.g., different part numbers or delivery dates) each with independent status histories.
The quantity of units (dies, wafers, or packaged devices) on the order line at the time of this status transition. Captured as a snapshot to support backlog quantity analytics across status states.

The semiconductor part number (IC, SoC, ASIC, or FPGA) associated with the order line at the time of this status event. Denormalized for audit trail completeness and historical accuracy even if the part number changes later.

Reference to a Product Change Notification (PCN) that triggered or is associated with this status transition. Relevant when an order status changes due to a product or process change notification issued to the customer.

The order status immediately before this transition event. Together with new_status, defines the state transition vector for lifecycle analytics and backlog management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entered|confirmed|allocated|wafer_started|in_fab|shipped|invoiced|cancelled — promote to reference product]

The semiconductor process technology node (e.g., 5nm, 7nm, 28nm, 65nm) used to fabricate the product on this order line. Captured as a snapshot at the time of the status event for process-level backlog analytics.

Unit of measure for the ordered quantity: EA (each/packaged unit), WFR (wafer), DIE (individual die), or LOT (production lot). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure configuration for semiconductor products.

Standardized reason code explaining why the status transition occurred (e.g., CUST_REQUEST, CAPACITY_HOLD, YIELD_FAIL, ALLOC_RELEASE, SHIP_CONFIRM, CANCEL_EOL). Sourced from SAP SD reason code configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for full enumeration]

Free-text narrative providing additional context for the status transition beyond the structured reason code. Captures escalation notes, customer communication references, or fab hold explanations entered by the user or system.

The customer-requested delivery date on the order line at the time of this status event. Snapshot preserved in the history record to track whether delivery commitments changed across status transitions.

Indicates whether the duration in the prior status exceeded the SLA target hours, constituting an SLA breach. True when duration_in_prior_status_hours > sla_target_hours. Supports escalation triggering and customer reporting.

The contracted or internally defined SLA target duration (in hours) for the order to remain in the prior_status before transitioning. Used to assess whether the transition was on-time or breached the SLA threshold.

The reference number of the source business document that triggered this status transition (e.g., a purchase order number, a delivery note number, an invoice number, or a MES lot release number). Provides cross-system traceability.

The unique event or change document identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., SAP Change Document number CDHDR.CHANGENR, Camstar transaction ID, or Salesforce case event ID). Enables end-to-end traceability from the Silver layer back to the system of record.
FK to order.sales_order.sales_order_id — Status history records belong to a specific sales order. Required for audit trail integrity.
The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the status transition occurred. This is the principal business event time for this EVENT_LOG record, distinct from record audit timestamps.

Classifies the nature of the status transition: forward progression through the order lifecycle, backward reversal (e.g., re-open), cancellation, or data correction. Supports lifecycle analytics and anomaly detection.

The operational system of record that originated the status change event. Distinguishes between manual user-driven changes and automated system-driven transitions (e.g., MES wafer start confirmation triggering wafer_started status). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAP_SD|MES_CAMSTAR|MES_SMARTFACTORY|SALESFORCE_CRM|AGILE_PLM|MANUAL|API_INTEGRATION — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The timestamp when this status history record was last modified in the data platform. In an ideal append-only event log this should rarely change; populated to support correction workflows and data quality monitoring.
The wafer start authorization number issued by the fab planning system when the order transitions to wafer_started status. Ties the commercial order to the physical manufacturing release in the MES.
Unique surrogate identifier for the delivery confirmation record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the delivery_confirmation data product.
Reference to the customer who received and confirmed the semiconductor shipment. Used for downstream invoice release and revenue recognition attribution.
Reference to the sales order associated with this delivery confirmation. Enables traceability from proof of delivery back to the originating customer order in the order-to-cash lifecycle.
Reference to the parent shipment record for which this delivery confirmation is being captured. Links the proof of delivery (POD) event back to the originating shipment.

The Advance Shipment Notice number from the customer's EDI 856 ASN acknowledgement message. Used to correlate the delivery confirmation with the inbound EDI transaction from the customer's warehouse management or ERP system.

The carrier's own proof-of-delivery document reference number as provided in the carrier POD data feed. Used to reconcile the internal delivery confirmation with the carrier's logistics records and resolve freight disputes.
The carrier's shipment tracking number associated with this delivery confirmation. Enables direct lookup in carrier portals for POD document retrieval and dispute resolution.

The communication channel through which the delivery confirmation was received from the customer or carrier. 'edi' = Electronic Data Interchange (EDI 856/861); 'portal' = customer self-service portal; 'api' = direct API integration; 'email' = email-based confirmation; 'manual' = manually entered by customer service representative.

Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for this delivery confirmation record. Communicated to customers and logistics partners as the official proof-of-delivery (POD) reference number. Format: DC- followed by 10 digits.

Current workflow state of the delivery confirmation record. 'pending' indicates awaiting customer acknowledgement; 'confirmed' indicates full receipt acknowledged; 'disputed' indicates customer raised a discrepancy; 'partially_confirmed' indicates partial quantity acknowledged; 'cancelled' indicates confirmation voided.

The quantity of semiconductor units (dies, packaged ICs, wafers, or reels) confirmed as received by the customer at the destination facility. May differ from shipped quantity in cases of shortage, overage, or partial delivery. Used as the basis for invoice quantity and revenue recognition.

The actual calendar date on which the customer confirmed physical receipt of the semiconductor shipment at the destination facility. This is the principal business event date for the delivery confirmation and serves as the trigger date for invoice release and revenue recognition per ASC 606 / IFRS 15.

Precise date and time at which the customer confirmed receipt of the shipment, including timezone offset. Used for SLA measurement, carrier performance analytics, and precise revenue recognition event logging.

Timestamp when this delivery confirmation record was first created in the system, either via carrier POD data feed or customer EDI 856 ASN acknowledgement ingestion. Audit trail field for data lineage.

The customer's formal acceptance decision for the delivered semiconductor product. 'accepted' = unconditional acceptance; 'rejected' = full rejection, return initiated; 'conditionally_accepted' = accepted with noted discrepancy pending resolution; 'pending_inspection' = customer incoming quality inspection in progress.

The customer's purchase order number as referenced on the delivery confirmation. Cross-references the customer's procurement system to the semiconductor manufacturer's order-to-cash records. Critical for invoice matching and accounts receivable reconciliation.

Name of the customer representative who signed off on the delivery confirmation at the receiving facility. Captured from carrier POD document or EDI 856 acknowledgement. Classified as confidential PII as it identifies an individual at the customer organization.

The reference identifier of the customer's authorized signoff on the delivery confirmation, such as a digital signature ID, customer dock receipt number, or customer ERP goods receipt document number. Required for invoice release authorization and revenue recognition event triggering.

Number of calendar days by which the confirmed receipt date exceeded the committed delivery date on the sales order. Negative values indicate early delivery. Zero indicates on-time delivery. Used for carrier performance scorecards and customer SLA compliance reporting.

Classification of delivery performance relative to the confirmed delivery date on the sales order. 'on_time' = delivered within the committed window; 'early' = delivered before committed date; 'late' = delivered after committed date; 'partial' = partial quantity delivered; 'failed' = delivery not completed. Used for OTIF (On-Time In-Full) KPI reporting.

The silicon revision or stepping of the delivered semiconductor device (e.g., A0, B1, C2). Captured at delivery confirmation to ensure the customer received the correct device revision as specified in the purchase order and product change notification (PCN).

Free-text narrative description of the discrepancy reported by the customer. Captures details not covered by the discrepancy_type enumeration, such as specific damage descriptions, lot number mismatches, or partial damage to a reel. Used in quality notification and RMA processing.

Boolean indicator set to TRUE when the customer has reported any discrepancy between the shipment contents and the delivery confirmation. Triggers downstream discrepancy resolution workflow, quality notification in SAP QM, and potential RMA initiation.

Classification of the discrepancy reported by the customer. 'quantity_shortage' = fewer units received than shipped; 'wrong_part' = incorrect part number or device type received; 'damaged_goods' = physical damage to ICs, packaging, or wafers; 'wrong_lot' = incorrect lot number or traceability mismatch; 'labeling_error' = incorrect label or marking; 'none' = no discrepancy.

Boolean indicator set to TRUE when export control compliance has been verified for this delivery, confirming the shipment reached the authorized end-destination and end-user as specified in the export license. Required for BIS/EAR and ITAR compliance audit trails.

The customer's ERP goods receipt document number (e.g., SAP MIGO GR document) confirming the semiconductor shipment has been posted into the customer's inventory. Provides the strongest form of delivery confirmation for invoice matching and dispute resolution.

The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code governing the transfer of risk and title for this shipment. Determines the point at which delivery is legally confirmed and revenue recognition is triggered. Critical for export compliance and financial reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Boolean indicator set to TRUE when this delivery confirmation has triggered the downstream invoice release event in SAP S/4HANA FI/SD. Enables monitoring of the order-to-cash cycle and identification of confirmed deliveries pending invoice generation.

The date and time at which the invoice release event was triggered in SAP S/4HANA FI following delivery confirmation. Used for order-to-cash cycle time analytics and revenue recognition timing compliance under ASC 606 / IFRS 15.

business_glossary_term: International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) Controlled
description: Boolean indicator set to TRUE when the delivered semiconductor product is subject to ITAR controls. Triggers mandatory end-use verification and delivery confirmation audit requirements under ITAR Part 123.

The semiconductor wafer lot number associated with the delivered product. Enables full traceability from delivery confirmation back to the fabrication lot in the MES (Camstar/Applied Materials SmartFactory). Critical for quality escapes, field failure analysis, and DPPM tracking.

The number of physical packages (boxes, reels, trays, tubes) confirmed as received by the customer. Compared against the shipped package count to identify missing packages in transit. Used for carrier claims and freight audit.

The manufacturer's part number (MPN) of the semiconductor product delivered. Used to verify correct part delivery against the customer PO and to cross-reference with the product master in Oracle Agile PLM and SAP MM.

The originating data source from which this delivery confirmation was captured. 'carrier_pod' = carrier electronic POD feed; 'edi_856' = customer EDI 856 ASN acknowledgement; 'customer_portal' = customer self-service portal submission; 'manual' = manually entered by customer service; 'email_confirmation' = email-based confirmation.

The arithmetic difference between shipped_quantity and confirmed_quantity (shipped minus confirmed). A positive value indicates a shortage; a negative value indicates an overage. Used to trigger discrepancy workflows and credit/debit memo processing in SAP S/4HANA FI.

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination country where the semiconductor shipment was received. Used for export control compliance verification, trade compliance reporting, and geographic revenue analytics.

The customer's facility or dock location code where the semiconductor shipment was physically received. Sourced from the customer EDI 856 ASN or carrier POD data. Used for logistics analytics and customer facility mapping.

Boolean indicator set to TRUE when this delivery confirmation has satisfied the performance obligation criteria and triggered a revenue recognition event in the financial system. Critical for SOX-compliant revenue reporting.

The Return Merchandise Authorization number issued when a discrepancy results in a product return. Populated only when discrepancy_flag is TRUE and a return has been authorized. Links the delivery confirmation to the downstream RMA and reverse logistics process.

Boolean indicator confirming that the delivered semiconductor product complies with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting hazardous substances. Captured at delivery confirmation for regulatory compliance documentation.
The quantity of semiconductor units originally shipped as recorded on the shipment record. Compared against confirmed_quantity to identify shortages or overages at delivery confirmation.

The unit of measure for the confirmed delivery quantity. EA = each (individual packaged IC or die); REEL = tape-and-reel packaging; WAFER = bare or processed wafer; TRAY = JEDEC tray; TUBE = IC tube packaging; LOT = wafer lot.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this delivery confirmation record, such as discrepancy updates, status changes, or customer signoff amendments. Supports audit trail and change tracking.

Reference to the freight carrier or logistics service provider assigned to transport this shipment. Used for carrier performance tracking and freight cost allocation.

Reference to the customer's destination ship-to location (customer site, distribution center, or consignment hub) for this delivery schedule line.
Reference to the parent sales order header from which this delivery schedule line originates. Links the schedule line back to the order-to-cash transaction header.

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Delivery schedule ownership is recorded for accountability in on‑time delivery performance reports.
Reference to the semiconductor product (IC, SoC, ASIC, FPGA, or packaged die) being scheduled for delivery on this schedule line.

The date on which the shipment was confirmed as received at the customer's ship-to location. Used for on-time delivery (OTD) KPI calculation and customer satisfaction reporting.
The actual date on which the shipment physically departed the ship-from location. Populated upon goods issue posting in SAP. Used for on-time shipment performance measurement.

Numeric priority rank assigned to this delivery schedule line during constrained supply allocation. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used by supply chain planners to allocate limited wafer or die inventory across competing customer orders.

Indicates whether this delivery schedule line is currently in backlog status (confirmed delivery date is past due or confirmed quantity is less than ordered quantity). Used for backlog management reporting and customer escalation tracking.

The specific call-off or release number issued by the customer against a blanket order for this delivery schedule line. Identifies the periodic release instruction within the blanket order framework.

The delivery date confirmed by the semiconductor manufacturer to the customer after availability check (ATP) and capacity validation. May differ from the requested date due to wafer fab cycle time or OSAT capacity constraints.

Unique identifier for the product errata record. Primary key.
Reference to the customer who first reported this errata, if discovered externally. Null if discovered internally. Used for customer relationship management (CRM) and design-win tracking.
Foreign key to the site where the errata was discovered.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Errata tracking assigns an engineering owner employee for root‑cause analysis and customer communication.
Reference to the affected integrated circuit (IC) product, system on chip (SoC), application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC), or field-programmable gate array (FPGA) product.
Reference to the automatic test equipment (ATE) test pattern, design verification plan test case, or simulation run that reproduces the errata. Used for regression testing and validation.
FK to product.ic_catalog.ic_catalog_id — Errata document bugs in specific IC products. Must reference the affected product and revision.
Comma-separated list of all silicon revisions affected by this errata (e.g., A0,A1,B0). Used to determine scope of customer impact.
Description of customer application scenarios or operating modes impacted by the errata (e.g., high-speed PCIe Gen4 operation, concurrent multi-channel DMA, low-power sleep modes).
Assessment of commercial and operational impact including potential revenue risk, customer satisfaction impact, warranty exposure, and time to market (TTM) implications.

Reason for closing the errata record. Fixed in Silicon: corrected in new revision; Fixed in Software: resolved via patch; Workaround Sufficient: mitigation acceptable; Product EOL: end-of-life product; Not Reproducible: cannot verify; Working as Designed: not a defect.
Timestamp when this errata record was first created in the product lifecycle management (PLM) system. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.

Status of customer communication regarding this errata. Not Disclosed: internal only; Pending Approval: awaiting management approval; Disclosed NDA: shared under non-disclosure agreement; Disclosed Public: published in public errata sheet.
Date when the errata was first communicated to customers via product change notification (PCN), errata sheet update, or technical advisory. Null if not yet disclosed.

Source or method by which the errata was discovered. Internal Validation: found during wafer testing or quality assurance; Customer Report: reported by design-in customer; Field Failure: observed in production deployment; Design Verification: identified during design for testability (DFT) or automatic test pattern generation (ATPG).
Date when the silicon bug or functional deviation was first identified, either during internal validation, customer testing, or field deployment.
Name or identifier of the design engineer or engineering team responsible for investigating and resolving this errata. Used for accountability and workload tracking.

Business identifier for the errata record, typically formatted as product-family prefix followed by sequential number (e.g., SOC-001234, FPGA-000567). Used in customer communications and product change notifications (PCN).

Current lifecycle status of the errata record. Draft: initial documentation; Under Review: engineering validation; Confirmed: verified silicon bug; Disclosed: communicated to customers; Resolved: fix available; Closed: no longer applicable.

Planned resolution approach for the errata. Next Silicon Revision: will be corrected in next tapeout; Software Patch: addressable via firmware/driver update; No Fix Planned: low priority or end-of-life (EOL) product; Under Evaluation: fix strategy being assessed.
Silicon stepping or revision where the errata will be corrected (e.g., C0, D1). Null if fix is software-only or no fix planned.
Name of the intellectual property (IP) core, register transfer level (RTL) module, or functional block where the errata occurs (e.g., PCIe Controller, DDR PHY, CPU Core, DMA Engine).
Estimated or actual number of customers affected by this errata based on product shipments and silicon revision distribution. Used for risk assessment and communication prioritization.
Confidential internal notes for engineering team use only. May contain sensitive technical details, customer names, or business strategy information not suitable for external disclosure.
User identifier or name of the person who last modified this errata record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this errata record. Used for change tracking and synchronization with downstream systems.
Reference to the product change notification (PCN) document number if this errata was formally communicated to customers. Null if disclosed through other channels or not yet disclosed.

Assessment of whether the errata affects compliance with industry standards or regulatory requirements such as automotive IATF 16949, functional safety ISO 26262, or export control ITAR/EAR classifications. Null if no regulatory impact.
Comma-separated list of related errata record identifiers that share common root cause, affect the same functional block, or have dependencies. Used for impact analysis and consolidated fixes.
Date when the errata was resolved either through silicon revision fix, software patch release, or closure decision. Null if still open or under evaluation.
Engineering analysis of the underlying silicon defect or design flaw causing the errata. May reference RTL logic error, timing violation, or process variation sensitivity.

Business impact severity of the errata. Critical: system failure or data corruption; Major: significant functional impact; Moderate: limited functional impact with workaround; Minor: cosmetic or edge-case issue.
Silicon stepping or revision identifier where the errata was discovered (e.g., A0, B1, C2). Corresponds to mask revision in the fabrication process.
Detailed technical description of the observable behavior or functional deviation caused by the silicon bug. Includes conditions under which the issue manifests.
Concise summary title of the silicon bug or functional deviation. Used in errata lists and customer-facing documentation.
FK to product.product_revision.product_revision_id — Errata are specific to a silicon revision/stepping. Must link to the affected revision.
Date when engineering verification of the errata was completed and status changed to verified or cannot reproduce. Null if verification still in progress or not started.

Engineering validation status of the reported errata. Unverified: initial report not yet investigated; In Progress: under engineering analysis; Verified: confirmed as silicon bug; Cannot Reproduce: unable to replicate reported behavior.
Indicates whether a software, firmware, or operational workaround exists to mitigate the errata impact. True if workaround documented; False if no mitigation available.

Detailed instructions for software, firmware, or operational workaround to avoid or mitigate the errata. May include register configuration changes, driver patches, or usage restrictions. Null if no workaround available.

Description of any power, performance, or area (PPA) degradation introduced by the workaround (e.g., 5% throughput reduction, 10mW additional power consumption, increased latency). Null if no measurable impact or no workaround.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Product families map to profit centers to attribute revenue and margin in financial statements.
Number of active PCNs currently in effect for this product family. Indicates ongoing changes to process, materials, or design that require customer notification and approval.

Specific application domain or use case this product family addresses (e.g., Advanced Driver Assistance Systems, Edge AI Inference, 5G Baseband Processing). Used for technical marketing and customer solution mapping.
Business unit or division responsible for this product family (e.g., Automotive Solutions, Mobile Computing, IoT and Embedded). Aligns with organizational structure and P&L responsibility.
Timestamp when this product family record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.
Primary design center location responsible for this product family (e.g., San Jose CA, Austin TX, Bangalore India). Used for resource planning and IP ownership tracking.

Business name of the product family (e.g., Cortex-M Series, Automotive MCU Family, RF Transceiver Family). Used in roadmap planning, marketing materials, and customer design-in targeting.

Name or identifier of the business authority who approved the LTB notification for issuance. Typically a product line manager, business unit director, or discontinuance review board representative.
Date and time when the LTB notification was approved for issuance. Establishes the audit trail for discontinuance governance and compliance with internal approval processes.

Explanation of why the LTB notification was cancelled. Provides business context for discontinuance reversals, such as renewed customer demand, supply chain recovery, or strategic product portfolio changes.

Name or identifier of the business authority who cancelled the LTB notification. Populated only if the notification is cancelled before completion, typically due to business strategy changes or supply chain recovery.
Date and time when the LTB notification was cancelled. Establishes the audit trail for notification lifecycle changes and supports customer communication of discontinuance reversals.
Date and time when the LTB notification record was first created in the system. Establishes the audit trail for notification lifecycle and supports compliance with record retention requirements.
Date by which customers must acknowledge the LTB notification if acknowledgment is required. Ensures timely customer response and enables proactive follow-up for non-responsive accounts.

Indicates whether customers are required to formally acknowledge receipt and understanding of the LTB notification. True for high-value or strategic customers; false for standard commercial notifications.
SSOT FK reference to canonical customer.customer_ltb_notification; this table is a secondary view and must not duplicate master data.
Primary business reason for product discontinuance and LTB issuance. Categorizes the strategic, operational, or compliance driver behind the End of Life (EOL) decision.
Detailed explanation of the business rationale for product discontinuance. Provides context for customers and internal stakeholders on the strategic or operational factors driving the EOL decision.
Projected revenue from LTB orders during the notification period. Used for financial planning, production capacity allocation, and business impact assessment of the product discontinuance.
Projected unit volume of LTB orders. Used for production planning, wafer start authorization, and supply chain capacity allocation during the LTB period.

Last date on which customers may place orders for the discontinued product. After this date, no new orders will be accepted. Critical milestone for customers planning bridge inventory or transitioning to replacement products.
Last date on which the discontinued product will be shipped to customers. Represents the absolute end of product availability and marks the transition to replacement product or alternative sourcing.
Name or identifier of the individual who issued the LTB notification to customers. Typically a product marketing manager, customer communications specialist, or PCN coordinator.
Date and time when the LTB notification was issued to customers. Marks the official start of the notification period and triggers customer acknowledgment and ordering timelines.
Date and time when the LTB notification record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to the notification and supports audit trail requirements for data lineage and compliance.
Maximum quantity that may be ordered during the LTB period. Caps total available inventory to prevent over-commitment and ensure fair allocation across customer base.
Minimum quantity that must be ordered during the LTB period. Ensures economically viable production runs for discontinued products and helps customers plan bridge inventory requirements.

Free-form text field for additional context, special instructions, or customer-specific considerations related to the LTB notification. Supports operational flexibility and captures information not covered by structured fields.
Primary communication channel used to deliver the LTB notification to customers. Tracks the method of notification delivery for compliance and audit purposes.
Date when the LTB notification was formally issued to customers. Marks the start of the official notification period and triggers customer acknowledgment requirements per JEDEC JESD48 standard.
Primary language in which the LTB notification was issued. Supports multi-lingual customer communication requirements and regulatory compliance in international markets.
Business identifier for the LTB notification. Externally communicated reference number used in customer correspondence and Product Change Notifications (PCN).
Current lifecycle status of the LTB notification. Tracks progression from draft through issuance, customer acknowledgment, and final expiration or cancellation.

Classification of the LTB notification based on timeline and business circumstances. Standard LTB follows normal discontinuance timelines; expedited LTB has shortened notice periods; extended LTB provides longer lead times; emergency discontinuance addresses immediate supply chain or compliance issues.
Reference to the associated Product Change Notification that formally communicates the discontinuance to customers. Links the LTB notification to the broader PCN process for traceability.

Indicates whether the product discontinuance requires regulatory approval or notification in specific jurisdictions. True for products subject to ITAR, EAR, or industry-specific regulations; false for standard commercial products.
Current status of regulatory approval for the product discontinuance. Tracks compliance with export control, defense, or industry-specific regulations that govern product lifecycle changes.

Indicates whether the replacement product requires customer qualification and design-in activities. True if the replacement is not a drop-in substitute and requires engineering validation; false if it is form-fit-function compatible.
SSOT reference to canonical customer.customer_ltb_notification (de-duplicates cross-domain concept).
Count of customers who have formally acknowledged the LTB notification. Enables tracking of acknowledgment completion rate and identification of customers requiring follow-up.
Count of unique customers who received the LTB notification. Tracks the breadth of customer impact and supports acknowledgment tracking and follow-up activities.

Reference to the semiconductor product (IC, SoC, ASIC, FPGA, IP core, or discrete device) undergoing qualification testing.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Qualification Program execution is overseen by a program manager employee; required for qualification status dashboards.
Foreign key to the site where the qualification program is conducted.
Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: Supplier Qualification Program tracks qualification status, audits, and risk for each supplier associated with a product line.
Pass/fail criteria for the qualification program, typically expressed as maximum allowable failure rate or zero failures for critical parameters.
Actual date when qualification program was completed and final report approved. Gate-keeps product lifecycle transition from NPI to volume production.
Actual date when qualification testing activities commenced.
Timestamp when the qualification program record was first created in the system.
Detailed description of any approved deviations from the standard qualification test plan, including technical justification and risk assessment.
Indicates whether any deviations from the standard qualification test matrix were granted by engineering review board.

business_glossary_term: Electrostatic Discharge (ESD) Charged Device Model (CDM) Test Enabled
description: Indicates whether ESD Charged Device Model stress test is included in the qualification plan. CDM simulates discharge from a charged device to ground.
Target voltage level in volts for ESD Charged Device Model testing, typically 500V minimum for Class C2 devices.

business_glossary_term: Electrostatic Discharge (ESD) Human Body Model (HBM) Test Enabled
description: Indicates whether ESD Human Body Model stress test is included in the qualification plan. HBM simulates discharge from human contact.
Target voltage level in volts for ESD Human Body Model testing, typically 2000V minimum for Class 2 devices.
Maximum allowable failure rate expressed in Defective Parts Per Million (DPPM) for the qualification program to pass.
Duration in hours for Highly Accelerated Stress Test, typically 96 to 264 hours.

Indicates whether Highly Accelerated Stress Test is included in the qualification plan. HAST combines high temperature, high humidity, and voltage bias to accelerate corrosion and moisture-related failures.
Relative humidity percentage for Highly Accelerated Stress Test, typically 85% RH.
Temperature in degrees Celsius for Highly Accelerated Stress Test, typically 110°C to 130°C.
Duration in hours for High Temperature Operating Life stress test, typically 1000 hours minimum per JEDEC standards.

business_glossary_term: High Temperature Operating Life (HTOL) Test Enabled
description: Indicates whether High Temperature Operating Life stress test is included in the qualification plan. HTOL accelerates time-dependent failure mechanisms under elevated temperature and voltage bias.
Junction temperature in degrees Celsius for High Temperature Operating Life stress testing, typically 125°C to 150°C.
Timestamp when the qualification program record was last updated.
Trigger current in milliamperes for latch-up testing, typically 100mA minimum per JEDEC standards.
Indicates whether latch-up susceptibility test is included in the qualification plan. Latch-up is a CMOS failure mode where parasitic thyristor structures create low-impedance paths.
Number of independent fabrication lots from which qualification samples must be drawn to ensure manufacturing process representativeness.
Planned date for completion of all qualification testing and final report approval.
Planned date for initiation of qualification testing activities.
Business identifier for the qualification program, typically combining product family code and qualification standard abbreviation.
Descriptive name of the qualification program including product identifier and target application domain.
Current lifecycle status of the qualification program. Gate-keeps product lifecycle transition from NPI to volume production.

Industry or customer-specific reliability qualification standard governing the test plan. JEDEC JESD47 for general semiconductor reliability, AEC-Q100 for automotive ICs, AEC-Q101 for discrete semiconductors, AEC-Q104 for multi-chip modules, MIL-STD-883 for military/aerospace, or customer-specific requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: JEDEC_JESD47|AEC_Q100|AEC_Q101|AEC_Q104|MIL_STD_883|CUSTOMER_SPECIFIC|IEC_60749|IEC_62196 — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Category of qualification program based on trigger event: new product introduction, process node change, package technology change, supply chain change, periodic requalification, or extended temperature range qualification.
Name of the reliability engineer responsible for planning, executing, and reporting the qualification program.
Total number of device units required for the complete qualification test plan across all test conditions and lots.
Number of temperature cycles for Temperature Cycling test, typically 500 to 1000 cycles minimum.
Indicates whether Temperature Cycling stress test is included in the qualification plan. TC accelerates thermal fatigue failures due to coefficient of thermal expansion mismatch.
Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius for Temperature Cycling test, typically 125°C to 150°C.
Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius for Temperature Cycling test, typically -55°C or -65°C.
Version identifier of the qualification test matrix document defining the complete set of reliability and environmental stress tests, sample sizes, and acceptance criteria.
Indicates whether a waiver was granted to allow production release despite qualification test failures or incomplete testing.
Business and technical justification for granting a qualification waiver, including risk mitigation plan and customer approval documentation.
